Multi-Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis

About the dataset

Primary description: Multi-ethnic atherosclerosis study

The Multi‐Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis (MESA) is a longitudinal study of subclinical cardiovascular disease that began in the year 2000. This population has diverse racial and ethnic characteristics, including 25% Black, 15% Chinese American, 19% Hispanic, and 41% White participants. The MESA cohort was sub-sampled to collect brain MR imaging at six sites at the time of the sixth exam, between the years 2018-2019.
Study Multi-Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis
Study Website MESA
Data Descriptor Paper https://doi.org/10.1161/JAHA.121.023159
DUA https://tools.mesa-nhlbi.org/MESA_Files/publications/Information%20for%20New%20Investigators.pdf
https://dbgap.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/aa/wga.cgi?stacc=phs000283.v1.p1&view_pdf
Country/Region USA
Disease Tag cardiovascular
Age (mean ± SD) 74.2 ± 8.2
Age Range 59-99
% Female 53.3
CUBIC Project /cbica/projects/MESA
PI for CUBIC Access Approval Christos Davatzikos
N Subjects 1062
N MR Sessions 1062
Diagnoses
AD
Value V01
CN 1041

Funding
